articles.title: Statistical Data Visualization
articles.contents:
Contents
Syllabus
Homework
Project
Readings

In this course, we’ll see how ideas from statistics and visualization can enhance our power of critical observation. We’ll develop a foundation for designing and implementing effective visualizations, and we’ll see how they can be integrated into the data science workflow, the day-to-day work of science.
It’s something of a paradox, but statistics has two contradictory roles in science,
Data summarization: Our brains are bad at processing large collections of numbers, but good at making visual comparisons. Statistics can compress a complicated dataset into a succinct representation, something that might be possible to visualize and hopefully wrap our heads around.
Data generation: In a way, the results of a statistical analysis are themselves new data. A standard error gives information about the stability of an estimate; the results of a randomized experiment give new information about hypothetical manipulations.
We’ll see these two themes throughout the semester, finding ways to navigate complex datasets and coaxing relevant data out of black boxes, for example.
Syllabus
The official syllabus, which gives requirements, learning objectives, and grading mechanisms, is available here.
Homework
Assignments will be due every other Friday. Submissions must be made on Canvas.
